name: Chaos Magic
description: Chaos Magic is a contemporary magical paradigm emphasizing practical results and individual experimentation over strict adherence to traditional systems. Emerging in the late 20th century, it rejects dogma and embraces the idea that belief itself is a tool to be wielded. Practitioners draw from diverse sources, including traditional occultism, science, psychology, and popular culture, creating personalized magical systems. Key tenets include the use of sigils, altered states of consciousness, and the deliberate manipulation of belief to achieve desired outcomes. Chaos Magic emphasizes gnosis, a state of heightened focus or trance, as a means to bypass the conscious mind and directly influence reality. It values effectiveness above all else, encouraging practitioners to adapt and innovate constantly. The cultural significance lies in its accessibility and empowerment of individuals to create their own spiritual paths.
subtitle: Belief as a Tool for Change

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Sigils
keyMyths.description: The myth of sigil creation involves condensing a desired outcome into a symbolic form and charging it with energy through gnosis. This process is believed to bypass the conscious mind and directly influence reality, aligning with the practitioner's will.

keyMyths.title: The Utilization of Belief Systems
keyMyths.description: Chaos Magic posits that any belief system can be temporarily adopted and utilized for magical purposes. This allows practitioners to tap into the power of various deities and spiritual entities without necessarily committing to a specific faith or tradition.

sources.author: Peter J. Carroll
sources.description: A foundational text outlining the principles and practices of Chaos Magic.
sources.text: A foundational text outlining the principles and practices of Chaos Magic.

sources.author: Phil Hine
sources.description: A practical guide to Chaos Magic, emphasizing experimentation and individual results.

sources.author: Dave Lee
sources.description: Explores the cultural and philosophical aspects of Chaos Magic, focusing on its subversive potential.
